name: House Shirsh

street_level_faction_presence.street_signals: Symbols, uniform cues, work marks, and rumor shorthand make power visible in daily movement.

greetings: Polite but probing introductions are common; people listen for evasion.
hospitality_rules: Hospitality exists, but questioning often begins before comfort is offered.
taboo_topics: boasting about unsanctioned magical tricks in public
class_expectations: investigators, scribes, and witnesses are all expected to keep their narratives coherent and exact
faith_etiquette: faith is secondary to compliance and truth-seeking procedure in many public spaces
outsider_mistakes: treating magical law as abstract when locals treat it as civic safety

occupation_and_daily_life.daily_rhythm: The everyday tempo is built from casework, witness traffic, evidence handling, magical anomaly review, and investigative rumor.
occupation_and_daily_life.work_rhythm_by_calendar_or_season: Celestial and anomaly periods can suddenly intensify work and public anxiety.
occupation_and_daily_life.ordinary_texture: People speak in lowered voices around cases. Ink, metal seals, burnt warding materials, and rain-damp stone shape the atmosphere.

travel_and_transition_texture: Arrivals feel assessed; travelers who carry magical goods or strange stories are likely to be remembered.
dwelling_and_settlement_life: Homes tend to separate guest space from work records, evidence lockers, or personal warding habits.
relationship_and_reputation_logic: People are remembered for whether their testimony holds, whether they respect procedure, and whether they bring danger with them.
region_specific_emotional_tone: Shirsh stories emerge as investigations, whispered threat spirals, truth tests, procedural pressure, and anxiety around what is really happening.
